free_text: Spectroscopy of 47K and proton core-excitations in 48Ca
from the 48Ca(t,alpha)47K reaction
Tritium beam.
Ten solid Delta-E - E telescopes
were used. Each telescope consisted of a 120 um
surface barrier silicon Delta-E detector with a 5 mm
lithium drifted silicon E-detector. Counting rates
were kept below 2 kHz/telescope and the resulting
dead time was less than 1%. Horizontal acceptance for
each telescope was +-0.3 degree.
4 solid-state detectors were used to monitor
the target.
362 um/cm2 target of 48Ca enriched to 93%.
Target thickness was determined by a normalization grid
on the elastic data and searching for the optimum
optical-model fit at forward angles.
Energy calibration of spectra at forward angles
included 15N peaks originating from 16O in the target.
Calibration provided the excitation energies of 47K
states used as calibration points for the backward
angle spectra. Each run was normalized using the
integrated beam current measured in a suppressed
Faraday cup.
Total error of absolute cross sections is
mainly due to the uncertainty in the target thickness
determination. This error is not included in the
angular distributions shown on figs.
No information about source of uncertainty
SD: Small corrections in all Subents.
ERR-T -> ERR-1.
